news.t: Inside HALX: Top Holdings, Weightings, and What Makes the ETF Unique
ETF Sequels: The Godfather Part II of New Launches?
New HALX ETF Bets Big on Tangible Assets & Cash Flows
Hard Assets, Low Obsolescence: Tuttle Capital Launches the HALX ETF (HALX)

holdings.infoBox: HALX is an equity ETF with a total of 41 individual holdings. The top holdings are Devon Energy stock at 4.59%, J.B. Hunt Transport Services at 3.39%, West Pharmaceutical Services at 3.34%, Old Dominion Freight Line at 3.26%, and CoreWeave at 2.95%.

overview.description: The Tuttle Capital Heavy Assets Low Obsolescence ETF (HALX) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund tracks an equal-weighted index of large-cap US companies identified as resilient to AI disruption. The fund selects firms with significant tangible assets and business characteristics associated with durable, asset-backed cash flows. HALX was launched on May 19, 2026 and is issued by Tuttle Capital.
infoTable: Tuttle Capital Heavy Assets Low Obsolescence Index

data.title: Inside HALX: Top Holdings, Weightings, and What Makes the ETF Unique
data.text: Launched on May 199, 2026 2026, the Tuttle Capital Heavy Assets Low Obsolescence ETF (HALX) is one of the newest entrants in the thematic ETF landscape.

data.title: ETF Sequels: The Godfather Part II of New Launches?
data.text: The pace of innovation in the ETF industry is hitting breakneck speeds. We have already seen more than 450 new launches in 2026, part of a massive wave of fresh products testing the waters.

data.title: New HALX ETF Bets Big on Tangible Assets & Cash Flows
data.text: On Tuesday, May 19, Tuttle Capital Management debuted the Tuttle Capital Heavy Asset Low Obsolescence ETF (HALX). This new fund from Tuttle Capital operates with a management fee of 75 basis points.
